'''jalala''' is an [[Aelaki]] [[Nouns|noun]] meaning "[[Wikt:okamisan|okamisan]] (auto-generated)". It has inherent '''male''' gender.

== Overview ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Root consonants
| j-l-l
|-
! Word class
| [[Noun]]
|-
! Gloss
| [[Wikt:okamisan|okamisan]] (auto-generated)
|-
! Inherent gender
| male
|-
! Citation form
| jalala
|}

== Inflected forms ==
{| class="wikitable sortable"
! Gender.Number !! 1st !! 2nd !! 3rd !! 4th
|-
| [[male]].[[collective]] || jælælæth || jælælæj || jælælæsh || jælælæ
|-
| [[male]].[[plural]] || jalalalath || jalalalaj || jalalalash || jalalala
|-
| [[male]].[[singular]] || jalalath || jalalaj || jalalash || jalala
|-
| [[male]].[[zero]] || jalaflafth || jalaflafj || jalaflafsh || jalaflaf
|}

''16 forms generated.''

== Case paradigm ==
''Shown for male singular.''
{| class="wikitable"
! Case !! 1st !! 2nd !! 3rd !! 4th
|-
| [[Agent]] || jalalatha || jalalaja || jalalasha || jalalaka
|-
| [[Patient]] || jalalath || jalalaj || jalalash || jalala
|-
| [[Possessive]] || jalalathal || jalalajal || jalalashal || jalalakal
|-
| [[Instrumental]] || jalalaathatha || jalalaajaja || jalalaashasha || jalalaaka
|-
| [[Dative]] || jalalathan || jalalajan || jalalashan || jalalakan
|-
| [[Speaker]] || jalalathaoro || jalalajaoro || jalalashaoro || jalalakaoro
|}

== See also ==
* [[Aelaki Lexicon]]

[[Category:Aelaki nouns]]
[[Category:Aelaki male nouns]]
[[Category:Aelaki vocabulary]]
[[Category:Aelaki lemmas]]
[[Category:Word pages]]
[[Category:Words c2e237e]]
{{wordpage|d176386}}
